responsibilities: Recording the order of legitimate and illegitimate descendants within royal kingdoms.
Managing the closeness or distance of relationships among all members of the imperial clan.
Receiving and processing annual nominal rolls and lineages submitted by commanderies and kingdoms.
Reviewing legal cases involving imperial clan members facing punishments of head-shaving (kun) or higher, and reporting them to the Emperor for final judgement.
historical_notes: During the Eastern Han Restoration (Zhongxing), the positions of Director and Assistant of the Grand Works Office (Du Sikong Ling/Cheng) under its jurisdiction were abolished.

title_en: Assistant to the Director of the Imperial Clan
responsibilities: Assisting the Director of the Imperial Clan in administrative and judicial duties.

personnel_count.notes: One administrator appointed per princess / 每主家令一人

responsibilities: Managing the domestic and financial affairs of a princess's household.
historical_notes: Subordinate staff under the Household Administrator increased or decreased without fixed quota.

personnel_count.notes: One assistant appointed per princess household / 每主一一人

responsibilities: Assisting the Princess Household Administrator in domestic management.
Managing all monetary and grain resources, including gold, silk, and various forms of currency.
Auditing monthly financial reports on current cash and grain balances submitted by commanderies and kingdoms on the first of each month.
Tracking and isolating outstanding financial debts and arrears in separate detailed statements.
Approving and dispatching resource allocations requested by border commanderies, balancing surpluses and deficits across the empire to ensure adequate supply.
historical_notes: Originally, salt and iron offices (yanguan, tieguan) in commanderies and kingdoms belonged to the Da Sinong, but during the Eastern Han Restoration (Zhongxing) they were transferred to local commandery and county jurisdictions. The Director of the Ritual Animal Corral (Linxi Ling), the Market Director of Luoyang, and the Ao Granary official of Xingyang were transferred to the Governor of Henan (Henan Yin). Other agencies, including the Balanced Transportation offices (junshu), were entirely abolished.

title_en: Assistant to the Minister of Finance
responsibilities: Assisting the Minister of Finance in central administrative and fiscal accounting duties.

title_en: Bureau Assistant to the Minister of Finance
responsibilities: Managing the central state treasuries, storehouses, and physical vaults.
Receiving, logging, and storing tribute grain transported via waterways from the commanderies and kingdoms.

title_en: Assistant Director of the Great Granary
responsibilities: Assisting the Director of the Great Granary in grain intake and logistics management.
Monitoring market and commodity prices to execute price stabilization operations.
Managing the boiling, refining, and dyeing of fabrics, and the production of colored textiles.
Assisting the Director of Price Stabilization in textile workshop operations and market surveillance.

title_en: Director of the Imperial Grain Processing Office
responsibilities: Supervising the hulling, milling, and selection of fine grain for imperial court consumption.
Overseeing the manufacture of dried travel provisions and emergency rations.

title_en: Assistant Director of the Imperial Grain Processing Office
responsibilities: Assisting the Director of the Imperial Grain Processing Office in mill operations.
Managing all items used by the Emperor for personal use, including robes, clothing, treasures, precious commodities, and delicacies.
Overseeing the massive infrastructure of inner court craft production, domestic service, secretariat documentation, and palace security details.
Collecting and managing imperial private revenues from natural resources (mountain, marsh, pond, and lake taxes) prior to fiscal restructuring.
historical_notes: The Shaofu historically held six central deputies (Cheng), five of which were abolished during the Eastern Han Restoration (Zhongxing). Emperor Shizu instituted sweeping structural updates: the 'forbidden money' taxes from natural resources were reassigned to the Ministry of Finance (Da Sinong); the Office of Works (Kaogong) was moved to the Grand Stables (Taipu); and the Water Management Bureau (Dushui) was delegated to local commanderies. The independent Office of the Director of Imperial Parks (Shuiheng Duwei, ranked Bi 2000 Shi) was entirely abolished and integrated directly into the Shaofu (only resurrected temporarily for a day during the annual autumn hunting rites). Additionally, 10 Shanglin pond supervisors, the directors and assistants of the Imperial Maternity Ward (Baorenchang), and 3 eunuch directors (Huanzhe, Kuntai, Cifei) along with 21 of their deputies were terminated. Conversely, from the Zhanghe era onward, the footprint of inner court eunuchs expanded, creating several concurrent or secondary supervisor roles (Changyao, Taiguan, Yuzhe, Goudun, Shangfang, Kaogong, Biezuojian) at the 600 Shi rank.

responsibilities: Assisting the Privy Treasury in managing overall palace administration, fiscal accounting, and bureau monitoring.

title_en: Director of the Imperial Medical Office
responsibilities: Supervising the corps of court physicians and managing medical treatments for the Emperor and the inner palace.
Procuring, inspecting, and managing physical drugs, raw herbs, and medicinal substances.
Recording, preserving, and checking medical formulas and complex imperial prescriptions.
Managing the preparation, cooking, and presentation of the Emperor's daily food, beverages, and ceremonial banquets.
historical_notes: The independent Office of the Soup Master (Tangguan Ling) was abolished during the restoration and consolidated as an assistant position under the Imperial Kitchen.

responsibilities: Overseeing primary food preparation, daily meals, and cooking operations.
Managing sweet delicacies, seasonings, and the maintenance of imperial dining utensils.
Supervising the brewing of wines, preparation of fine beverages, and herbal soups.
Sourcing and managing fresh and dried fruits for imperial tables and sacrifices.
Managing personal imperial office materials, including paper, ink, writing brushes, and sealing clay.
Supplying and coordinating administrative physical assets and accounting materials required by the Central Secretariat.
Assisting the Director in organizing stationery distribution and material auditing.
Managing the flora, fauna, and wild animals within the imperial Shanglin hunting preserve.
Exercising administrative jurisdiction over civilian farming communities residing inside the borders of the park.
Securing captured game and transferring wild beasts to the Imperial Kitchen.

title_en: Assistant Director of the Shanglin Park
responsibilities: Assisting in park logistics, forestry management, and residential oversight.
Enforcing security, policing boundaries, preventing poaching, and maintaining safety within the park.
Eunuch post. Managing the accounts, registers, and domestic activities of harem concubines (Honorable Ladies and Chosen Women).
Assisting in the general administration and logistics of the harem side courts.
Assisting in the domestic registries and management of the harem side courts.
Supervising medical treatment and quarantine facilities for sick women within the inner palace.
Operating the disciplinary confinement and detention chamber for Empresses or concubines found guilty of legal infractions.
Eunuch post. Directing and supervising the massive corps of female palace servants, slaves, and operational maids.

title_en: Assistant Director of the Palace Lanes
responsibilities: Assisting in organizing and deploying female labor across palace projects.
Eunuch post. Managing the workshop production, tailoring, mending, and washing of textiles and apparel for the Emperor's household.
historical_notes: The independent Office of the Weaving Room (Zhishi Ling) was abolished during the restoration and consolidated as an assistant under this bureau.

title_en: Assistant Director of the Imperial Wardrobe
responsibilities: Assisting in the storage and upkeep of imperial garments.
Supervising female artisans engaged in weaving, embroidering, and fabricating fine cloth.
Eunuch post. Setting up and conducting minor ancestral offerings, household sacrifices, and specific seasonal religious rites within the interior palace walls.
Assisting in religious preparations and storing sacrificial instruments.

title_en: Director of Imperial Parks and Ponds
responsibilities: Eunuch post. Overseeing the maintenance, structural upkeep, and security of urban canals, detached gardens, imperial lakes, and leisure resort complexes located around the capital.

title_en: Assistant Director of Imperial Parks and Ponds
responsibilities: Assisting in core administrative accounting and park labor tracking.

title_en: Assistant for the Yong'an Palace Park
responsibilities: Managing the detached Yong'an palace estate and its gardens situated northeast of the Northern Palace.
Managing detached pavilions, resting villas, and inner lounges built across the gardens.
Supervising cultivation, harvesting, and maintenance within designated palace orchards.
Managing the imperial Hongchi estate and reservation located twenty li east of Luoyang.
Managing the plants and landscaping of the Southern Garden situated south of the Luo River.
Directly inspecting the Zhuolong garden reservation positioned immediately adjacent to the Northern Palace.
Directly inspecting the Zhili park situated within the southwest corner of Luoyang.
Managing and guarding the Emperor's private cash reserves, fine silks, gold, silver, and precious material goods.

title_en: Assistant Director of the Inner Treasury
responsibilities: Assisting in regular stockaudits, ledger recording, and storehouse security.

title_en: Director of the Inner Wardrobe Ward
responsibilities: Managing, unpacking, presenting, and properly arranging raw cloth fabrics and fine finished garments for the imperial apartments.
Coordinating Left wardrobe displays and cloth inventories.
Coordinating Right wardrobe storage and garment logs.
Supervising master artisans in manufacturing the Emperor's highly specialized ceremonial blades, longswords, and decorative artifacts.

title_en: Assistant Director of the Imperial Workshop
responsibilities: Assisting in manufacturing safety, supply allocation, and evaluating artistic work.
Attending directly on either side of the Emperor, guiding ongoing tasks, and providing elite advice and rapid responses to verbal inquiries.
Providing a highly educated co-rider (Can-cheng) to accompany the Emperor inside his formal carriage during full processions, while other attendants follow behind on horseback.
historical_notes: Originally headed by a Leader (Puye). During the Eastern Han Restoration, this role was converted into a Libationer (Jaijiu), which was intermittently filled or omitted.

personnel_count.notes: Eunuch, no fixed quota / 宦者，無員

responsibilities: Eunuch post. Serving in constant personal attendance on the Emperor, following him directly into the innermost apartments, facilitating administrative and domestic tasks, and serving as closer verbal consultants.
historical_notes: The salary tier was originally 1000 Shi, but was upgraded to a rank equivalent to Erqianshi (Bi 2000 Shi).

title_en: Gentleman Attendant at the Yellow Gate
responsibilities: Attending close to the imperial presence, handling central information pathways, and acting as standard document liaisons between the inner chambers and outer bureaucracy.
Guiding feudal vassal kings and foreign envoys to their assigned places upon their entry into formal throne room audiences.

title_en: Junior Attendant at the Yellow Gate

personnel_count.notes: Eunuch, no fixed quota / 宦者，無員

responsibilities: Eunuch post. Running daily documents, messages, and approvals between the inner palace and the Central Secretariat.
Relaying formal communication across the inner court, the Empress's apartments, and outer departments.
Dispatched by imperial decree to check on health conditions and convey sympathy whenever princesses or princely queen mothers fell ill.
Eunuch post. Acting as the administrative chief and controller of all operational eunuchs stationed within the palace.

title_en: Assistant Director of the Yellow Gate
responsibilities: Assisting the Director in maintaining discipline and logging assignments for eunuch personnel.

title_en: Retinue Assistant of the Yellow Gate
responsibilities: Managing the order, alignment, and movement of the eunuch attendants who escort the imperial carriage on tours.

title_en: Chief of the Yellow Gate Quarters
responsibilities: Eunuch post. Supervising domestic activities and personnel deployment within assigned zones of the Empress's residence.

title_en: Chief of the Painted Chamber Quarters
responsibilities: Eunuch post. Managing operations inside designated sections of the inner empress palace complex.

title_en: Chief of the Jade Hall Quarters
responsibilities: Eunuch post. Directing maintenance and safety within the designated Jade Hall rooms of the harem.
Eunuch posts. Managing seven specific residential quarters and chambers inside the Empress's apartments.

title_en: Supervisor of Retinue Attendants of the Central Yellow Gate
responsibilities: Eunuch post. Commanding the unassigned retinue attendants and guards of the Central Yellow Gate.
Providing personal bodyguards and door protection inside the palace; serving as armed equestrian escorts flanking the imperial chariot during outdoor travels.

personnel_count.notes: Eunuch, no fixed quota / 宦者，無員

responsibilities: Eunuch post. Executing basic services, message running, and cleaning inside the restricted zone of the forbidden palace.
historical_notes: The position was originally ranked at Bi 100 Shi, but was upgraded to Bi 300 Shi.

responsibilities: Managing administrative official appointments, processing incoming court memorials, and checking executive paperwork across all Secretariat bureaus.
historical_notes: Inherited from the Qin dynasty system. Emperor Wu filled this post with eunuchs, renaming it the Director of the Palace Secretariat (Zhongshu Yezhe Ling). Emperor Cheng returned the role to scholar-officials and restored its original title.

responsibilities: Assisting the Secretariat Director; taking full charge of document submission and processing all state items whenever the Director is absent.

personnel_count.notes: One per section / 曹各一人

responsibilities: Heading the six functional administrative departments (Cao) of the central state: Changshi Cao (handles elite ministers), Erqianshi Cao (split into two sections for provincial administrators), Mincao (handles petitions from minor officers and citizens), and Kecao (split into Southern and Northern Guest sections for foreign diplomacy).
historical_notes: Emperor Cheng initially created four Secretaries for four Cao. Emperor Shizu preserved the system but split the Erqianshi Cao into two distinct bureaus, and further split the Kecao into the Southern Guest Section (Nanzhuke Cao) and Northern Guest Section (Beizhuke Cao), creating the final six-department structure.

responsibilities: Recording administrative records, logging incoming paperwork, and scheduling executive meetings.
Managing petitions and official reports submitted by lower bureaucrats and commoners; controlling the dispatch of postal couriers and scribes.
Authorizing the use of official seals, distributing sashes, and allocating office materials (paper, ink, brushes) and checking treasury stores.
Drafting official documents, polishing state paperwork, and composition of imperial decrees within their assigned departments.

personnel_count.notes: Originally 18 (3 per section), 3 added later for urgent business / 本十八人，後增劇曹三人

responsibilities: Executing core record-keeping, manual transcription, and clerical tasks for their respective sections.
Heading the Tally and Insignia Bureau (Fujie Tai); managing state tallies, imperial credentials, and identification tokens.
Issuing formal credentials and travel authorizations whenever imperial envoys are dispatched.

title_en: Gentleman in Charge of Seals and Tallies

personnel_count.notes: Originally 2 stationed inside the palace / 舊二人

responsibilities: Holding and protecting the imperial seals of state, military tiger tallies, and bamboo verification tallies.
Handling clerical logs and recording verification token issues.
Serving as active head of the Palace Censorate (Yushi Tai). Monitoring palace activities, auditing central and regional departments, and secretly investigating and impeaching illegal actions.
Acting as absolute supervisor of conduct, decorum, and ritual etiquette enforcement during major state sacrifices, throne room assemblies, and peerage ceremonies.
historical_notes: Originally established as the immediate deputy to the Grand Censor (Yushi Dafu). When the Grand Censor's office was changed into the Minister of Works (Sikong), this post remained in the palace as the head of the Censorate, and was subsequently placed under the Shaofu.

responsibilities: Recruited from individuals expertly trained in statutory codes. Evaluating complex legal appeals, disputed lawsuits, and doubtful criminal cases forwarded from across the empire to determine legal accuracy based on codified statutes.
Investigating government operations to detect infractions, receiving official denunciations filed against state ministers, and presenting dynamic impeachments against misconduct.
Providing on-site protocol surveillance during state ceremonies.

title_en: Clerk of the Orchid Terrace Archives
responsibilities: Managing archival histories, receiving and archiving executive memorials, and documenting manufacturing operations and seal logs.
